title: Sudan War Enters Fourth Year with Devastating Toll
detail: As the Sudan civil war marks its fourth year, new casualty figures reveal the devastating human cost of the conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and Rapid Support Forces (RSF). Over 150,000 people have been killed since fighting began in April 2023, while 14 million Sudanese have been displaced from their homes. The conflict has created one of the world's largest humanitarian crises, with widespread famine conditions and the collapse of basic services across much of the country.

media.caption: Sudan conflict enters its fourth year with massive humanitarian toll
